steps.title: Apply Moore's common sense
steps.content: Text 1 says Moore believed all people instinctively know certain things, including that they have bodies and exist in a three-dimensional world. External world skepticism denies certainty about anything outside one's own mind.

steps.title: Reject responses Moore would not make
steps.content: A says Moore is unserious, which Text 1 does not suggest. B focuses on skeptics' distaste rather than Moore's own argument. C treats instinctive rejection of a proof as equivalent to Moore's common-sense beliefs. The answer is D.
